title: Digitization of Industrial Inspection Sheets by Inferring Visual Relations
Method and System for Information Extraction from Document Images using Conversational Interface and Database Querying

content:
The aim of this work is to develop a system that would automatically extract relavant information from images of graphs viz. bar graph, pie chart, line diagram. The idea for this project was conceived as we received an use case that involved understanding webpages by visually impaired people. Although, with the modern OCRs, they are easily able to comprehend the textual content in a page, understanding graphical information, which sometimes contain valuable information is still a challenge. As a resolution for the same, we wanted to come up with a system by which the visually impaired people can ask questions of an image and get all the necessary information in form of answers to those questions.
Our present work is essentially composed of the following -
  A reasoning-based neural module that attends to the informative areas in an image. This module leverages the information in the question to do the same.
  These questions can have answers of multiple types ( for ex., generic answers like yes, no, none, names etc. and image specific answers which are present as text in the image, like label of the highest bar ).
  We treat generic answers as a classification problem and regress the bounding boxes around image specific answers like labels of graphs.
  A gating mechanism is used to select the regression or classification branch based on the question.
  Our model is an end-to-end differentiable neural network capable of answering questions of multiple types by attending to relavant areas in an image.
  As a fundamental flaw from a practical perspective is to know which questions to ask, we are now looking into automating the question asking process so that the system can ask the necessary questions and summarize the image.
summary: Visual Understanding of graph images by automatic answering of reasoning-based questions
title: Reasoning over Graph Images
An Efficient End-to-End Neural Model for Handwritten Text Recognition
Reading Industrial Inspection Sheets by Inferring Visual Relations
Deep Reader: End-to-end framework for relevant text extraction from real image documents with Natural Language Interface

content:
Developed a novel framework for automatic digitization of inspection sheets that contain schematics of factory floor, handmarked by engineers. It intelligently combines various traditional image processing methods with an ensemble of deep neural networks to achieve the goal.
For more details, refer to the “paper”.
As this was a part of a client project, the entire codebase cannot be made public. However, my Tensorflow implementations of the Capsule Network that was used in this work, is available here. And my Tensorflow implementation of the CNN based Digit Classifier Network, is available here.
summary: Developed a novel framework for automatic digitization of industrial inspection sheets.
title: Digitization of Industrial Inspection
Systems and Methods for End-to-End Handwritten Text Recognition using Neural Networks

content:
The aim of this project was to develop an end-to-end model for handwritten text recognition from scanned documents. The main components of the model are -
  A convolutional feature extractor that generates a sequence of visual features from an image of a handwritten line of text.
  A Sequence-to-Sequence model that consists of a bidirectional LSTM based encoder and a decoder with attention to generate a set of characters present in the image.
  For more details, refer to the “paper”
summary: Developed an end-to-end connectionist model for handwritten text recognition from document images.
title: Handwritten Text Recognition

content:
The aim of this work was to develop a system that would be able to verify faces in streaming data, like the video feed of a security camera. This is especially useful for enterprises to regulate entry into their offices. The idea is that a company should be able to verify the faces in a video feed with the faces of its known employees and an alarm can be raised if an unauthorized person enters the premises. The system is designed as follows -
  A haar-cascade face detector is used to detect faces in each frame in a video. We use the openCV implementation for the same.
  We use a pre-trained model of FaceNet architecture to extract embeddings for each face detected.
  Similar embeddings are generated for user provided face database that consists of one example face image per person to be verified in the video.
  One-Shot Similarity measure is used to determine the identity the face(s), if any, per frame by comparing the current embedding with all the embeddings in the database.
  The code for the same is available here
summary: Developed a face recognition/verification system for faces in wild.
title: Deep Face Recognition

content:
In an attempt to automate information retrieval from document images, my team and I have successfully developed a deep learning based platform called Deep Reader, that learns relationships among various entities in an image to efficiently extract relevant information from it. Essentially, it consists of -
  An Natural Language interface that allows users to enter their query. For example, one may ask for the account number printed on a scanned invoice.
  A set of vision APIs that extract visual information from the scanned doucment images. These include line, box and table detection, handwritten text detection and recognition, OCR on printed text and denoising and deblurring of degraded images of documents.
  Then, all the essential entities that are extracted ( for ex., receipt number, account information, names etc ) and stored in a database.
  The NL query by the user is then mapped to a set of SQL queries using a sequence - to - sequence model which are used to extract the relevent information.
  For more details, refer to the “paper”
summary: Contributed to the development of a deep learning based platform for image information retrieval.
title: Deep Reader

content:
As a part of my Master’s project, I implemented a set of algorithms for clustering of streaming data. This work consists of the following -
  A C++ implementation of incremental k-means clustering scheme that pre-clusters a set of high-dimensional data points into ellipsoidal partitions.
  A C++ implementation of hierarchical extension of the previous method.
  A C++ implementation of online Density Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise ( DBSCAN ) that uses the cluster centers generated by the pre-clustering method to add and streaming data points in the existing clusters.
  A detailed description of the algorithms can be found here.
The code is available here.
summary: Developed a method for hierarchical clustering of streaming data.
title: Incremental Clustering

content: This work develops a distributed fault tolerant area coverage algorithm, resulting in quick detection of the faulty agent under limited communication constraints and redistributes the area without conflicts.
summary: Analysed Multi-Agent area coverage problem for surveillance purposes.
title: Distributed Fault Tolerant Multi-Robot Area Coverage Under Limited Communication Ranges

content: Developed a web application, that takes input from the user about his/her preferences about the specification of the machine which include - operating system, main memory space, disk(storage) space, number of cores. Then we return the IP of the machine (Virtual Machine) assigned according to the mentioned preferences, for a particular amount of time. We use KVM for VM management.
summary: Cloud based Virtual Machine management system using KVM.
title: Creating Cloud of Local Servers

content: In this project we developed an IPv4 packet generator to simulate any network traffic the user wants. We interfaced this through a web application to provide user the provision for customizing source and destination IPs and ports,the protocol to be used and amount of that data each of the packets carry. We analyzed the most used protocols and identifying clogging points in network.
summary: Network traffic analysis and IPv4 packet generation for custom simulation.
title: Network Traffic Generator and Packet Analyzer

content: A novel technique to convert a captured image of a vegetation to a 3D model using L-Systems, a grammar for procedural vegetation modelling.
title: Procedural Tree Modelling Using L-Systems

content: This project proposes to build a smart campus infrastructure. A 3D interactive and immersive virtual/mixed reality environment will be designed to support geospatial services including smart navigation and telepresence. Two key aspects of the system are 3D modeling and rendering.
summary: Creating a virtual walk-through of IIIT-Delhi campus.
title: Virtual Campus Project
